sections.title: SECTION 1 LISTENING COMPREHENSION

parts.title: Part A
parts.instruction:
Directions: In Part A you will hear short conversations between two people. After each conversation, 
you will hear a question about the conversation. The conversations and questions will not be 
repeated. After you hear a question, read the four possible answers in your test book and choose the 
best answer. Then, on your answer sheet, find the number of the question and fill in the space that 
corresponds to the letter of the answer you have chosen.

questions.passage:
(woman) Are the exams corrected yet?
(man)No, but they'll be corrected by noon.
questions.question: 1. WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: He'll correct the exams this afternoon.
The exam will be at noon.
He will collect the exams at 12:00.
The tests will be graded by noon.

questions.passage:
(woman) Has Martha's visa arrived yet?
(man)I think it arrived last month.
questions.question: 2. WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: Martha applied for a visa last month.
Martha's visa will last for only a month.
Martha arrived last month without her visa.
One month ago Martha got her visa.

questions.passage:
(man)What did the professor do in the first lass? I missed it because I was late.
(woman) She outlined the course requirements.
questions.question: 3. WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

options.text: The professor described what the students should do.
There was a long line to register for the required class.
It is a requirement for each professor to teach at least one course.
The professor required the class to prepare an outline.

questions.passage:
(woman) How did Chuck look when you visited him in the hospital?
(man)He's looked better.
questions.question: 4. WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: Chuck had improved.
This visit was better than the last.
Chuck looked at him in the hospital.
Chuck didn't seem to be doing very well.

questions.passage:
(man)How much was tuition increased for next month?
(woman) More than I can afford.
questions.question: 5. WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

options.text: She thinks the tuition should be raised.
The semesters tuition is quite affordable.
She doesn't have enough money for her school fees.
She has more than enough for tuition.

questions.passage:
(woman) How were the grades on the history exam.
(man)No one got above a C.
questions.question: 6. WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: He thinks he got a good grade.
The history grades were all C or above.
No one got history grades.
There were no high scores.

questions.passage:
(woman) You know, this is the second time this week that you've been late to class!
(man)It was impossible to find a place to park before the ten o'clock class!
questions.question: 7. WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: The parking lots were full before 10:00.
It was impossible to start class by 10:00.
He parked the car before class at 10:00.
The possibility of finding a place to park increased.

questions.passage:
(man)Can I help you find something?
(woman) Yes, thank you. I need to get a new rug.
questions.question: 8. WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

options.text: She's found a new ring.
She needs to help him find something.
She's shopping for a carpet.
She's thankful she has a rag.

questions.passage:
(woman) I'd like to open an account.
(man)Would you like a savings account or an interest-bearing checking account?
questions.question: 9. WHERE DOES THIS CONVERSA TION PROBABLY TAKE PLACE?

options.text: In a department store.
In a bank.
In an accounting firm.
In a checkout line.

questions.passage:
(woman) Why does Jane spend so much time in San Francisco?
(man)She has a cousin there, so she likes to visit, especially during the holidays.
questions.question: 10.WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: Jane usually visits San Francisco for her vacations.
Jane's cousin often visits San Francisco.
Whenever there's a holiday, Jane's cousin goes to San Francisco.
Whenever there's a holiday, Jane leaves San Francisco.

questions.passage:
(woman) Are you really hungry?
(man)I feel like I haven't eaten in weeks.
questions.question: 11.WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: He'd really like to have something to eat.
Because he's weak, he can't eat.
It's been weeks since he's had anything to eat.
He hasn't felt like eating for weeks.

questions.passage:
(man)The traffic outside is really loud!
(woman) I'll say!
questions.question: 12. WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

options.text: Traffic should not be allowed.
She thinks that the traffic should stay Outside.
She agrees that the traffic is noisy.
She'll stay outside with the man.

questions.passage:
(woman) Have you seen the headlines yet
today?
I haven't had a chance to read a word.
questions.question: 13.WHAT ARE THEY PROBABLY DIS CUSSING?

options.text: The headings for today's reading assignment.
The chance to make the headlines.
Her reading ability.
The daily newspaper.

questions.passage:
(man)I'm not ready yet, and it's going to take me a while longer.
(woman) You'd better hurry. Take five minutes too long, and you'll miss the bus.
questions.question: 14. WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

options.text: The bus trip is only five minutes long.
The man missed the bus by five minutes.
The man should hurry to catch the bus.
The bus was five minutes late.

questions.passage:
(man)I think it's impossible for me to pass this class.
(woman) You should never say "impossible."
questions.question: 15. WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

options.text: It's not possible to pass the class.
She'll definitely fail.
It's always possible.
She shouldn't say anything about the class.

questions.passage:
(Man)  Why were you thanking Tom?
(Man)  He lent me enough money to pay the rent.
questions.question: 16.WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

options.text: She gave Tom money to pay the rent.
She was given money for the rent.
Tom borrowed money for the rent.
She had some money to lend.

questions.passage:
(woman) Are you enjoying the dessert?
(man)Never have I tasted such delicious  cake!
questions.question: 17.WHAT DOES THE MAN SAY ABOUT THE CAKE?

options.text: The cake is extremely good.
He never tasted the cake.
He wished he hadn't tasted the cake.
The cake has never been very good.

questions.passage:
(man)Why are you so late getting here?
(woman) Oh, I ran into my cousin Carl, and we  stayed and talked for a while.
questions.question: 18.WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

options.text: At the corner she ran into another car.
She ran to Carl because she cared.
She unexpectedly met one of her relatives.
Carl was running from place to place.

questions.passage:
(woman) Do you know where Debbie is?
(man)Her purse is still here, so she must still  be in the apartment.
questions.question: 19.WHAT DOES THE MAN SAY ABOUT DEBBIE?

options.text: She shouldn't leave her purse here.
She's probably in the apartment.
Her purse must not be in the apartment.
She left without taking her purse.

questions.passage:
(man)Do you know when rent is due?
(woman) The landlord collects it on the first of the month, without fail.
questions.question: 20.WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

options.text: The landlord failed to collect rent on the first of last month.
The tenants absolutely must pay rent at the beginning of the month.
The landlord will not fail to collect your rent on the first of next month.
It is important to call the landlord about rent on the first of the month.

questions.passage:
(woman) My car is making some funny noises.
(man)Why not take it to a mechanic?
questions.question: 21.WHAT DOES THE MAN SUGGEST TO THE WOMAN?

options.text: Taking the car out for a test drive.
Listening to the noises.
Fixing the car herself.
Getting the car repaired.

questions.passage:
(man)Martha's holding down two jobs at the same time.
(woman) She'd better take it easy.
questions.question: 22.WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

options.text: Martha s jobs are easy.
It's easy to hold two jobs.
It's better for Martha to have two jobs.
Martha should slow down.

questions.passage:
(woman) Did you get to the airport in plenty of time?
(man)There was scarcely enough time to get
there.
questions.question: 23.WHAT DOES THE MAN IMPLY?

options.text: The plane took off just after he arrived.
He arrived just after the plane took off.
He wasn't in time to catch the plane.
He arrived too late to catch the plane.

questions.passage:
(woman) You should put some money in the
(man)parking meter.
Parking fees aren't necessary on the
weekend, are they?
questions.question: 24.WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: He agrees with the woman's suggestion.
Parking is not free on the weekend.
It is not necessary for them to park.
He thinks they don't have to pay.

questions.passage:
(MAN) How is your boss feeling about his retirement?
(WOMEN) Oh, he isn't too unhappy to be retiing.
questions.question: 25. WHAT DOES THE WOMAN IMPLY ABOUT HER BOSS?

options.text: He is eager to leave his job.
He is unhappy at the thought of retiring.
He couldn't be unhappier about retiring.
He is retiring too soon.

questions.passage:
(MAN) Oh, I see you have a new car.
(WOMEN) Oh, he isn't too unhappy to be retiing.
questions.question: 26. WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: He got the car he really wanted.
He didn't get a new car.
The car that he got was not his first choice.
He didn't really want a new car.

questions.passage:
(WOMEN)Did you hear the president's announcement this morning?
(MAN) Yes, the president appointed Mr. Drew head of the newly formed commission.
questions.question: 27. WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: Mr. Drew pointedly asked the president about the committee.
The president pointed to Mr. Drew's head.
Mr. Drew became head of the new commission.
Mr. Drew was committed to the president's appointments.

questions.passage:
(MAN) Were you upset by what Richard said to vou?
(WOMAN) I couldn't have been more infuriated!
questions.question: 28. WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

options.text: She felt inferior.
She wasn't furious.
She felt there should have been more fairness.
She was extremely angry.

questions.passage:
(MAN) Let me just get these last plates put away. Then, I'll be ready to go.
(WOMAN) So you did do the dishes.
questions.question: 29. WHAT HAD THE WOMAN ASSUMED?

options.text: The man would do the dishes.
The plates did not need to be washed.
The man would not be ready to go.
The dishes would not be done.

questions.passage:
(WOMAN) Why did you get that kind of fruit?
(MAN) I wouldn't have bought these cherries had I known that grapes were so cheap.
questions.question: 30. WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: He knew that grapes were cheaper than cherries.
He didn't know that grapes were cheaper than cherries.
He bought grapes because they were cheaper than cherries.
He didn't buy either grapes or cherries because of the price.

sections.instruction: In this section of the test, you will have an opportunity to demonstrate your ability to understand conversations and talks in English. There are three parts to this section. Answer all the questions on the basis of what is stated or implied by the speakers you hear. Do not take notes or write in your test book at any time. Do not turn the pages until you are told to do so.
